exercises.name: 1.5-Mile Run (AF PT Test)
exercises.simplyPut: A battle-tested military exercise used by elite warriors. 1.5-Mile Run (AF PT Test) builds combat-ready fitness, mental toughness, and functional strength in your legs.

mistakes.consequence: Hypoglycemia, heat casualty, rhabdomyolysis
mistakes.correction: Minimum 500ml water and light meal 60-90 min before PT

mistakes.error: Ignoring pain to not appear weak
mistakes.consequence: Minor injury becomes career-ending; stress fracture becomes complete fracture
mistakes.correction: Report injuries early — 2 weeks light duty beats 6 months recovery; it's not weakness, it's tactical

mistakes.consequence: Blisters, hot spots, shoulder nerve compression, back pain
mistakes.correction: 80% weight on hips, 20% on shoulders; adjust straps every 30 min; break in boots

mistakes.error: Sleep deprivation as badge of honor
mistakes.consequence: Cognitive impairment (worse than 0.08% BAC after 24h), injury risk 2x
mistakes.correction: Sleep is a force multiplier — rest when mission allows; strategic naps 20 min

mistakes.consequence: Heat stroke (potentially fatal), HAPE/HACE at altitude
mistakes.correction: 14-day heat acclimatization; staged altitude ascent; know warning signs

physiologyLinks.effect: Extreme training can chronically elevate cortisol — periodization critical
Load carriage increases tibial bone density but risk of stress fractures
Severe caloric deficit during selection reduces testosterone significantly
10-14 day protocol increases plasma volume 10-15%, reduces core temp

pre_workout.why_avoid: Military training demands 4000+ kcal/day. Inadequate fuel leads to injury, overtraining, and rhabdomyolysis.

foods_to_avoid: Nothing — eat anything available during Hell Week
during_workout.why_avoid: During BUD/S Hell Week, ANY calories are better than none. Starvation is the enemy.

foods: Whatever is available — quantity over quality during intense training
post_workout.recovery_note: Military training: recovery nutrition is survival. 1.5g protein/kg/day minimum. Sleep and calories are the two most important recovery tools.

foodsPositive.benefit: Optimal carb:fat:protein ratio for sustained output
foodsPositive.mechanism: Mixed macros provide both rapid (dried fruit) and sustained (nuts) energy substrates

foodsPositive.benefit: Portable protein (33g/100g) for field conditions
foodsPositive.mechanism: Complete amino acids maintain muscle protein synthesis during prolonged physical stress

foodsPositive.benefit: Prevent hyponatremia during extreme sweat loss
foodsPositive.mechanism: Sodium (300-500mg/hr) maintains plasma osmolality and prevents cerebral edema

foodsPositive.benefit: Calorie-dense field nutrition (588 kcal/100g) with complete amino profile
foodsPositive.mechanism: High MUFA content provides sustained energy; arginine supports NO for circulation under load

foodsNegative.impairment: Constipation, micronutrient gaps, morale impact
foodsNegative.mechanism: Low fiber + preservatives alter gut microbiome; insufficient variety
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Iron supplements + MRE constipation = severe GI issues

foodsNegative.impairment: Anxiety, tremor, impaired fine motor skills under stress
foodsNegative.mechanism: Excessive sympathetic activation impairs steady-state performance
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Go-pills (modafinil) + excessive caffeine = insomnia, cardiac strain

foodsNegative.impairment: Giardia, crypto — mission-ending GI illness
foodsNegative.mechanism: Protozoan contamination; chlorine tablets required for all non-tested sources
foodsNegative.drug_interaction: Antibiotics may be needed; doxycycline is standard field prophylaxis

exercises.name: Army Push-Up (ACFT Standard)
exercises.simplyPut: Do as many push-ups as possible in 2 minutes following Army standards — chest touches ground, arms fully extend. Simple? Yes. Easy when you're on push-up number 47 and your arms are shaking? That's when character shows. This is the Army Combat Fitness Test standard.
exercises.howTo:
1. Start in front leaning rest position (high plank, arms extended)
2. Lower body until chest contacts the ground
3. Release hands briefly from floor (Hand Release Push-Up for ACFT)
4. Replace hands and press up to full arm extension
5. Body must remain rigid — no sagging hips or piking
6. Resting is only allowed in the front leaning rest (up position)
7. 2-minute time limit — pace yourself
8. Strategy: sets of 10-15 with 3-5 second rests, then grind singles in final 30 seconds

exercises.name: Dead-Hang Pull-Up (USMC PFT)
exercises.simplyPut: Hang from a bar with fully extended arms and pull yourself up until your chin clears the bar — no kipping, no swinging, just raw upper body strength. This has been the Marine Corps standard since 1956. Maximum score is 23 reps. It separates those who can carry their own weight from those who can't.
exercises.howTo:
1. Grip bar, palms facing away (overhand)
2. Start from dead hang (arms fully extended)
3. Pull up until chin clears bar
4. Lower with control to dead hang
5. No kipping or swinging
6. PFT: max reps

exercises.name: Special Ops Obstacle Qual
exercises.simplyPut: A battle-tested military exercise used by elite warriors. Special Ops Obstacle Qual builds combat-ready fitness, mental toughness, and functional strength in your full body.

mistakes.consequence: Hypoglycemia, heat casualty, rhabdomyolysis
mistakes.correction: Minimum 500ml water and light meal 60-90 min before PT

mistakes.error: Ignoring pain to not appear weak
mistakes.consequence: Minor injury becomes career-ending; stress fracture becomes complete fracture
mistakes.correction: Report injuries early — 2 weeks light duty beats 6 months recovery; it's not weakness, it's tactical

mistakes.consequence: Blisters, hot spots, shoulder nerve compression, back pain
mistakes.correction: 80% weight on hips, 20% on shoulders; adjust straps every 30 min; break in boots

mistakes.error: Sleep deprivation as badge of honor

mistakes.correction: Sleep is a force multiplier — rest when mission allows; strategic naps 20 min

mistakes.consequence: Heat stroke (potentially fatal), HAPE/HACE at altitude
mistakes.correction: 14-day heat acclimatization; staged altitude ascent; know warning signs
